title: stachys recta subsp. tenoreana
intro: Stachys recta subsp. tenoreana is a subspecies of the Stachys recta plant, also known as the straight wormwood or upright hedge nettle, which is a flowering plant in the mint family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Italian and Sardinian medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stachys recta subsp. tenoreana include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves and flowers of the plant.
